text_preview:
Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) & Cloud Architect
Professional Summary
Results-driven Site Reliability Engineer with 5 years of hands-on experience designing, scaling, and
securing highly available cloud infrastructures for product-based enterprise platforms. Deep expertise in
establishing robust SLIs, SLOs, and Error Budgets to bridge the gap between feature velocity and system
stability. Proven

work_experience.title: Lead Sre
bullets: Execution: Architected a fully automated, active-passive Multi-Region Disaster Recovery architecture. Utilized Terraform to duplicate VPC and EKS infrastructure in a secondary region. Implemented Velero to continuously back up Kubernetes state and persistent volumes to cross-region replicated S3 buckets. Configured Route53 latency-based routing with automated health check failovers.
Measurable Impact: Successfully reduced the platform’s Recovery Time Objective (RTO) from 4
Context: The engineering team suffered from extreme alert fatigue due to fragmented monitoring

certifications.name: integration initiative using Karpenter, successfully slashing monthly AWS compute expenditures by $45,000
IAM database credentials and rotating Kubernetes secrets, saving the engineering team 20+ hours weekly.
Context: The platform was highly vulnerable to single-region AWS failures (e.g., us-east-1 outages),
Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) - Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF)
AWS Certified DevOps Engineer - Professional - Amazon Web Services
AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate - Amazon Web Services
